---
label: "Advanced Tool Patterns"
icon: "🔧"
---

## Advanced Tool Patterns

Production MCP tools go well beyond simple request-response. This section covers tool chaining, idempotency, partial failure handling, content types beyond text, and designing tools that LLMs use reliably at scale.

      <CodeBlock b64="" />
      <ExpandableSection title="Idempotency and Safe Tool Design" icon="🔄">

LLMs may retry tool calls if they do not see a response. Write-tools must be idempotent to prevent duplicate side effects.

        <CodeBlock b64="" />
      </ExpandableSection>
      <ExpandableSection title="Tool Chaining via Continuation Tokens" icon="🔗">
        <CodeBlock b64="" />
      </ExpandableSection>
      <Quiz question="An LLM calls your 'send_email' tool and does not receive a response before the timeout. It retries the call. What happens if your tool is not idempotent?" options={["Nothing — the LLM knows not to retry write operations","The email is sent twice","The server rejects the duplicate automatically","Timeouts never happen in production MCP"]} correctIndex={1} explanation="Without idempotency, a retried tool call will execute the full side effect again. For email sending, this means duplicate emails. For payment processing, duplicate charges. Every write-tool in production MCP must either be idempotent by nature or implement deduplication via idempotency keys."/>
